rationale: The recent runs look architecture-limited and nearly flat under training, so I kept the winning 9x544 backbone and added a cheap token-level residual head that can learn fast within the short step budget. A learned unigram bias plus a full vocab-sized bigram table directly targets next-token statistics, adds little compute relative to the existing output projection, and is explicitly preserved as fp16 in the final artifact so the new signal survives the int8+zlib roundtrip that actually determines score.
title: Residual bigram/unigram logit head kept fp16 under quantization
